grants.name: Enterprise Development Grant (EDG)
grants.description: Core grant for Singapore companies to upgrade, innovate, and internationalise. Supports projects in three pillars: Core Capabilities, Innovation & Productivity, and Market Access. Covers consultancy, training, certification, and equipment costs.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% of qualifying costs. Typically S$30K–S$1M per project.

grants.name: Productivity Solutions Grant (PSG)
grants.description: Adopt pre-approved IT solutions and equipment to improve productivity. Sector-specific solutions (retail, F&B, logistics, construction) and cross-sector solutions (CRM, ERP, HRMS, accounting, cybersecurity, data analytics).
grants.amount_note: Up to 50% of qualifying costs (70% for advanced solutions like AI/robotics). Per solution per year.

grants.name: Market Readiness Assistance (MRA)
grants.description: Help SMEs expand overseas through support for market setup, business matching, in-market promotion, and partner identification. Covers market assessment, legal/documentation, and market entry costs.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% of qualifying costs, capped at S$100K per company per new market. Max 2 applications per market.

grants.name: Enterprise Financing Scheme (EFS)
grants.description: Government-backed low-interest loans across 7 areas: Trade Loan, Working Capital Loan, Fixed Assets Loan, Project Loan, Venture Debt, M&A Loan, and Green Loan. Government shares 50–70% risk with banks. Working capital up to S$500K, trade up to S$10M, projects up to S$50M.
grants.amount_note: Working Capital: up to S$500K. Trade: S$10M. Project/M&A: up to S$50M. Government risk-share 50–70%.

grants.name: Sustainability Reporting Grant
grants.description: Co-funds first sustainability report for SMEs. Covers consultancy for ESG disclosure aligned with international standards. Prepares businesses for regulatory and market ESG requirements.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% co-funding for first sustainability report, capped at S$35K.

grants.name: Enterprise Sustainability Programme (ESP)
grants.description: Build sustainability capabilities and capture green economy opportunities. Covers sustainability workshops, playbooks, capability development, and project funding. Supports ESG reporting readiness and green transformation.
grants.amount_note: Playbook/strategy: up to 70% co-funding. Project implementation via EDG sustainability track.

grants.name: Senior Employment Credit (SEC)
grants.description: Automatic wage offset for employers hiring Singaporean workers aged 55+ earning ≤ S$4,000/month. Offsets up to 8% of wages for workers aged 67+. Reduces employer CPF burden. No application needed — auto-disbursed.
grants.amount_note: Wage offset: age 55–59 (2%), 60–64 (3%), 65–66 (5%), 67+ (8%). Automatic — no application needed.

grants.name: CPF Transition Offset
grants.description: Government offset to help employers manage CPF rate increases for senior workers aged 55–70. Automatically covers 50% of the increase in employer CPF rates. Transitional through 2030.
grants.amount_note: Automatic offset: covers 50% of employer CPF rate increases for workers aged 55–70. Phased through 2030.

grants.name: Enabling Employment Credit (EEC)
grants.description: Wage offset for employers hiring Singaporean persons with disabilities earning ≤ S$4,000/month. Offsets up to 20% of wages. Encourages inclusive hiring. Automatic disbursement.
grants.amount_note: Up to 20% wage offset for hiring persons with disabilities. Automatic disbursement.
eligibility: Employer of Singaporean persons with disabilities

grants.name: Part-time Re-employment Grant
grants.description: Grant for employers implementing part-time re-employment policies for senior workers transitioning to retirement. Covers restructuring, training, and policy implementation. Up to S$125K per company.
grants.amount_note: Base S$25K + additional per senior worker on part-time. Max S$125K per company.

grants.name: Career Conversion Programme (CCP)
grants.description: Salary support + training subsidy for reskilling mid-career workers (including PMETs, mature workers) for new job roles. Up to 90% salary support during training + course fee subsidies.
grants.amount_note: Up to 90% salary support (capped S$6K/month × 6 months = S$36K). Course fees subsidised 70–90%.
eligibility: Hiring for new role with reskilling

grants.name: Work-Life Grant
grants.description: Supports implementation of Flexible Work Arrangements (FWAs) and enhanced leave schemes. Covers consultancy, infrastructure, and HR policy development.
grants.amount_note: FWA Grant: S$70K + Enhanced Leave Grant: S$35K = max S$105K.

grants.name: SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit (SFEC)
grants.description: One-off S$10,000 credit for employers to offset 90% of out-of-pocket costs for enterprise and workforce transformation. Covers SkillsFuture courses, business transformation consultancy, job redesign.
grants.amount_note: One-off S$10K credit. Covers 90% of qualifying costs. Valid for limited period.

grants.name: Training Grant Scheme (TGS)
grants.description: Course fee subsidies for employer-sponsored training. Covers all SSG-supported courses across industries. Base 50–70% for SMEs, enhanced up to 90% for SMEs and mature workers (40+). Absentee payroll support included.
grants.amount_note: SME course fee subsidy: 70% base, up to 90% enhanced. Absentee payroll support available.

grants.name: SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme (SCTP)
grants.description: Train-and-place programme for mid-career individuals transitioning to new sectors. Employers can tap trained talent or sponsor existing employees. Covers tech, healthcare, green economy, advanced manufacturing.
grants.amount_note: Trainee course fee subsidies 90–95%. Employers benefit from ready-trained talent pipeline.
eligibility: Employers hiring from or sponsoring through programme

grants.name: NTUC Company Training Committee (CTC) Grant
grants.description: Grant for companies forming Company Training Committees to drive workforce transformation. Covers training, job redesign, and productivity improvements recommended by the CTC. Union-employer partnership.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% funding for transformation projects + absentee payroll support.
eligibility: Unionised or willing to partner NTUC

grants.name: SMEs Go Digital Programme
grants.description: Flagship digitalisation programme. Pre-approved solutions with PSG support. Industry Digital Plans for 20+ sectors. Go Digital Advisor for personalised recs. Covers cybersecurity, data analytics, e-commerce, CRM, ERP, HR, accounting.
grants.amount_note: Up to 50% PSG support. Per pre-approved solution per year.

grants.name: Digital Leaders Programme
grants.description: For SMEs ready for advanced digitalisation. Supports integrated solutions across multiple business functions. Deeper funding beyond standard PSG for multi-function digital transformation.
grants.amount_note: Higher co-funding tier for advanced digital transformation. Tailored support beyond PSG.

grants.name: GenAI Navigator
grants.description: NEW (2026): Helps SMEs adopt Generative AI. Provides AI readiness assessment, curated GenAI solution recommendations, implementation support, and PSG co-funding. Assessment and advisory free.
grants.amount_note: PSG co-funding up to 50% for qualifying GenAI solutions. Assessment free.

grants.name: Open Innovation Platform (OIP)
grants.description: Crowd-sourcing platform matching business challenges with tech solvers. Post problems; solvers propose. IMDA co-funds prototype development. Covers AI, IoT, blockchain, data analytics.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% co-funding for prototype development. Challenge-based.

grants.name: AI Singapore Grants (100E Programme)
grants.description: National AI programme: 100 Experiments (100E). Companies propose AI use cases; AISG provides engineering talent + co-funding for POC development. NLP, computer vision, predictive analytics, recommendation systems.
grants.amount_note: AISG provides AI engineers + co-funding up to S$250K per 6–12 month project.
eligibility: AI use case with business impact

grants.name: Accreditation@SGTech
grants.description: Accreditation for high-growth Singapore tech product companies. Opens government procurement and enterprise contract doors. Credibility badge for expansion.
grants.amount_note: Non-monetary: government procurement facilitation, enterprise credibility, growth support access.

grants.name: Startup SG Founder
grants.description: First-time entrepreneur grant: S$50K (S$20K upfront + S$30K on milestones) + 12-month mentorship with Accredited Mentor Partners. Must co-match S$10K.
grants.amount_note: S$50K grant: S$20K initial + S$30K milestones. Co-match S$10K required.

grants.name: Startup SG Tech
grants.description: Fast-track grant for deep-tech startups commercialising proprietary tech. POC: up to S$250K. POV: up to S$500K. Covers development, testing, commercialisation.
grants.amount_note: POC up to S$250K. POV up to S$500K. Co-funding basis.

grants.name: Startup SG Equity
grants.description: Government co-investment matching private investment in startups. General tech: up to S$4M total. Deep tech: up to S$8M. Government matches 70% for general tech.
grants.amount_note: General tech: up to S$4M. Deep tech: up to S$8M. Government matches private investment.

grants.name: Startup SG Accelerator
grants.description: Funding for incubators/accelerators nurturing high-growth Singapore startups. Supports programme operations, mentorship, and startup grants.
grants.amount_note: Amount varies by programme scope and duration.

grants.name: Enterprise Innovation Scheme (EIS)
grants.description: 400% tax deduction on qualifying innovation expenditure: R&D in Singapore, IP registration, IP acquisition/licensing, SSG-aligned training (up to S$400K per activity/YA). Innovation projects with polytechnics/ITE: 400% on up to S$50K/YA. NEW (Budget 2026): 400% on qualifying AI expenditure up to S$50K/YA (no cash payout for AI). Option to convert up to S$100K of total qualifying expenditure to cash at 20% conversion rate. Active YA 2024-2028.
grants.amount_note: 400% deduction on first S$400K per activity/YA. Cash conversion: up to S$100K/YA at 20% rate (increased from S$20K). AI: 400% on S$50K/YA (no cash option). Active YA 2024-2028.

grants.name: Double Tax Deduction for Internationalisation (DTDi)
grants.description: 200% tax deduction on overseas market expansion expenses. Covers overseas trips, trade fairs, marketing collateral, business development. Auto-approved up to S$150K/YA.
grants.amount_note: 200% deduction on first S$150K/YA (auto-approved). Above requires ESG approval.

grants.name: Energy Efficiency Grant (EEG)
grants.description: Supports F&B, retail, and manufacturing businesses to adopt energy-efficient equipment: air-con, refrigeration, cooking equipment, LED, industrial motors/compressors. Reduces energy costs while greening operations.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% of qualifying costs. Cap varies by sector.

grants.name: Agri-Food Cluster Transformation (ACT) Fund
grants.description: Supports local farms and food businesses for productive, climate-resilient, resource-efficient capabilities. Technology adoption, innovation, R&D. Supports Singapore's 30-by-30 food security goal.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% co-funding. Multi-million tier for large-scale transformation.

grants.name: Tourism Development Fund (TDF)
grants.description: Supports tourism businesses to innovate, create new products, improve productivity. Covers tourism product development, events, digital transformation, sustainability, workforce upskilling.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% co-funding. Larger grants for high-impact tourism products.

grants.name: Maritime Cluster Fund (MCF)
grants.description: Supports maritime companies: business development, productivity, manpower, innovation. Covers training, internships, tech adoption, internationalisation, sustainability.
grants.amount_note: Co-funding 50–70% depending on track.

grants.name: SG Eco Fund
grants.description: Projects advancing environmental sustainability: waste reduction, resource efficiency, environmental outreach, circular economy, green community initiatives.
grants.amount_note: Up to 80% co-funding for qualifying sustainability projects.

grants.name: MICE Sustainability Grant
grants.description: MICE businesses adopting sustainable practices: sustainability consultancy, certification, green venue upgrades, waste reduction tech for events.
grants.amount_note: Up to 70% co-funding. Capped per project.
